CREATE TABLE users (id UInt32, name String, age UInt32);
SELECT id, name, age, age + 10 AS age_in_10_years FROM users WHERE age > 28;
CREATE TABLE orders (order_id UInt32, user_id UInt32, amount Float32);
SELECT u.name, SUM(o.amount) AS total_spent FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id GROUP BY u.name HAVING total_spent > 100;
SELECT name, age, age / 2 AS half_age FROM users WHERE name LIKE 'A%' AND age BETWEEN 20 AND 40;
CREATE TABLE products (product_id UInt32, price Float32, category String);
SELECT category, COUNT(*) AS product_count, AVG(price) AS average_price FROM products GROUP BY category HAVING average_price > 25;
SELECT u.name AS user_name, p.category AS product_category FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id LEFT JOIN products p ON o.amount > p.price WHERE u.age > (SELECT AVG(age) FROM users);
SELECT (CASE WHEN age > 30 THEN 'Senior' ELSE 'Junior' END) AS age_group FROM users;
CREATE TABLE sales (sale_id UInt32, amount Float32, sale_date Date);
SELECT SUM(amount) OVER (PARTITION BY sale_date ORDER BY sale_id) AS cumulative_sales FROM sales;
WITH total_spending AS (SELECT user_id, SUM(amount) AS total FROM orders GROUP BY user_id) SELECT u.name, ts.total FROM users u JOIN total_spending ts ON u.id = ts.user_id WHERE ts.total > 100;
CREATE TABLE events (event_id UInt32, user_id UInt32, event_time DateTime);
SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) AS event_count FROM events GROUP BY user_id HAVING event_count > 1;
